title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: A panel announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety 2026) in Glasgow, UK. Fabio Giglietto is listed as a featured panelist to discuss academic researchers' experiences and challenges using the Meta Content Library (MCL) following the shutdown of CrowdTangle.
description: An announcement for the #SMSociety conference panel featuring Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert speaker discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: An article on Lumsanews explaining Giglietto's investigation of 2.5 million Facebook posts. It details how the study's data highlights that extremist and conspiracy-related pages successfully bypassed Meta's political content restrictions, becoming the top source of political information on the platform by total visibility.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An article in the Italian online newspaper Open reporting on a study by Fabio Giglietto. The research shows that Meta's political content reduction policy on Facebook has severely suppressed the reach of mainstream, democratically elected politicians while inadvertently boosting extremist and conspiratorial content. The findings have sparked legislative discussions in Italy.
description: A news article reporting on a study by Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, which details how Facebook's algorithm suppresses mainstream political content while boosting extremist and conspiratorial reach.
